era_context: Late Akkadian period; Gutian incursions ongoing. Egyptian Old Kingdom in final phase. Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase. Traditional Xia Dynasty in early phase. Bronze Age established.

events.title: Akkadian political fragmentation continues
events.description: The Akkadian Empire continues its dissolution. City-states reassert independence; Gutian pressure persists. The cuneiform record shows minimal central authority. This period represents the tail end of Akkadian hegemony before Gutian dominance becomes established.
events.certainty_note: General process well attested; specific year-by-year events not documented.

events.title: Egyptian Sixth Dynasty ends; First Intermediate Period begins
events.description: The Sixth Dynasty of Egypt ends, and the First Intermediate Period (c. 2184–2055 BCE) begins. This marks the end of the Old Kingdom and the beginning of a period of political fragmentation. Multiple dynasties (Seventh through Tenth) rule simultaneously in different regions of Egypt. The exact date of this transition is debated; some scholars place it at c. 2184 BCE, others at c. 2170 BCE. The transition is marked by the loss of central authority and the rise of regional powers.
key_figures: Last Sixth Dynasty pharaohs (names and order disputed)
events.certainty_note: The transition from Old Kingdom to First Intermediate Period is well documented, but the precise date is debated (±20–30 years). The Turin King List provides a framework, but its absolute chronology depends on external synchronisms.
cross_references: Marks the end of the Old Kingdom
Begins the First Intermediate Period (c. 2184–2055 BCE)

events.title: Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization continues in its mature phase. Urban centres are fully developed and prosperous. Trade networks extend to Mesopotamia and the Persian Gulf. The civilization shows remarkable stability and cultural uniformity. No major disruptions are evident in the archaeological record.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Indus Valley sites

events.certainty_note: The stability of the Indus Valley Civilization during this period is well established through archaeology.
cross_references: Part of mature Indus phase (c. 2600–1900 BCE)

disconfirming_evidence: The date of the transition from the Old Kingdom to the First Intermediate Period is debated. Some scholars place it at c. 2184 BCE, others at c. 2170 BCE or even later. The chronology of the Sixth Dynasty is contested; some scholars argue for a 'low chronology' that would place these events 50–100 years later. The Sumerian King List's reliability is disputed.
historiographic_note: This year is poorly documented. The entries represent the continuation of broader trends rather than specific events. Chronological uncertainty is substantial (±30–50 years). The historical record for this year is essentially a blank.

graph_edges.note: First Intermediate Period precedes Middle Kingdom
